# Question Bank

Reusable elicitation questions organized by category. Pull from here when
building an interview guide or workshop agenda. Add to it whenever a new
question proves useful across engagements.

## Context & Goal

- What problem are you trying to solve right now?
- What does success look like in six months?
- What would happen if we did nothing?
- Who benefits most when this works?

## Current State

- Walk me through how this is done today, step by step.
- Where do you spend the most time?
- Where do mistakes most often happen?
- What workarounds do people use?
- What systems or tools are involved today?

## Pain & Friction

- What is the worst part of the current process?
- What have you tried that did not work?
- What part wakes you up at night?

## Future State & Vision

- If you could change one thing immediately, what would it be?
- What does great look like?
- What would your team stop doing if this worked?

## Stakeholders & Influence

- Who else is affected by this?
- Who needs to approve a change to this?
- Whose work would change downstream?

## Constraints

- What rules, policies, or regulations apply?
- What budget, time, or staffing constraints exist?
- What technical constraints do you know about?
- What must we not change?

## Volume & Frequency

- How often does this happen?
- How many <items> per day/week/month?
- What is the peak load?
- How long does each <item> take today?

## Exception Handling

- What happens when <edge case>?
- What is the worst case you have seen?
- How do you recover today?

## Non-Functional

- How fast must this be from the user's view?
- Who must be able to access this? Who must not?
- What data is sensitive? What retention applies?
- What availability is expected? What downtime is acceptable?
- What does accessible mean for your users?
- What languages, regions, or currencies must we support?

## Integration

- Where does the data come from today?
- Where does the data need to go?
- Who else consumes this output?
- What format is expected at the boundary?

## Success & Measurement

- How will you know this worked?
- What metric would your leadership look at?
- What number would change if this succeeded?

## Risk Surfacing

- What is the riskiest assumption we are making?
- What could go wrong that we have not discussed?
- What did you not tell me that I should have asked?

## Closing

- Who else should I talk to?
- What document or screen would help me understand this faster?
- What questions did I not ask that I should have?

## Anti-Questions (avoid)

These shapes lead the witness or restrict to yes/no.

- "Wouldn't it be better if ...?" — leading.
- "Do you want X or Y?" — false binary.
- "Is it okay if we do X?" — sign-off disguised as discovery.
- "How would you build this?" — asks the stakeholder to do the BA's job.
